original_hypothesis.title: Admin Can Hijack Vesting Allocations by Changing Marketplace Address
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: The setMarketplace function allows the admin to change the authorized marketplace address at any time. A malicious admin could point this to a custom contract, allowing them to drain all user vesting allocations held in escrow.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The VestingManager trusts the marketplace address implicitly via the onlyMarketplace modifier. If the admin is compromised or turns malicious, they can bypass all user consent and extract the entire TVL of listed vestings.

proof_of_signal.pattern: marketplace = _marketplace; protected only by onlyAdmin, no timelock

preconditions: Admin key is compromised or admin acts maliciously

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: Immediate state change of trusted caller without delay
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Attacker calls completePurchase from fake marketplace to assign vestings to themselves
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: 100% extraction of all listed vesting TVL

economic_questions: Is the admin a multisig or a single EOA?
Are there off-chain monitoring systems to detect this?
validation_requirements: Verify that completePurchase does not validate payment, only caller identity

market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: When TVL is at its peak

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
